name: DFS — Compare Node by Node
summary: Walk both trees together; at each position compare the two nodes (existence + value) and recurse into matching children, stopping early on any difference.
visualizationIntent: init: build both trees — p on top, q below. loop: pop a matching (p, q) position off the parallel stacks; the two cursors land on the corresponding nodes in each tree. check: compare the pair — both missing (ok), one missing (structure differs → error), or values differ (→ error). update: on a match, push the two left children and the two right children so the walk descends both trees in lockstep. return: show whether the trees are the same; a mismatch flashes the offending pair red.

lineExplanations.3: Start the list of tree p's nodes.
lineExplanations.4: Start a lookup from id to node for tree p.
lineExplanations.6: child-slot walker for p, just past the root.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over p's slots to build its nodes.
lineExplanations.8: Skip null slots — only real values become nodes.
lineExplanations.9: Create this p node with empty (None) child links.
lineExplanations.10: If a slot is left for a left child…
lineExplanations.12: link it as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.14: If a slot is left for a right child…
lineExplanations.16: link it as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.18: Add the finished node to p's list.
lineExplanations.19: Record it in p's id lookup.
lineExplanations.20: Start the list of tree q's nodes.
lineExplanations.21: Start a lookup from id to node for tree q.
lineExplanations.23: child-slot walker for q, just past the root.
lineExplanations.24: Loop over q's slots to build its nodes.
lineExplanations.26: Create this q node with empty child links.
lineExplanations.27: If a slot is left for a left child…
lineExplanations.29: link it as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.31: If a slot is left for a right child…
lineExplanations.33: link it as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.35: Add the finished node to q's list.
lineExplanations.36: Record it in q's id lookup.
lineExplanations.37: Assume the trees are the same until proven otherwise.
lineExplanations.38: done flips true the moment we find a difference.
lineExplanations.40: A stack of p positions still to compare.
lineExplanations.41: A parallel stack of the matching q positions.
lineExplanations.42: Seed with p's root (or None if p is empty).
lineExplanations.43: Seed with q's root (or None if q is empty).
lineExplanations.44: cur_p is the p node being compared (None = missing).
lineExplanations.45: cur_q is the q node being compared (None = missing).
lineExplanations.46: Keep comparing while positions remain and no difference was found.
lineExplanations.47: Take the next p position off the stack.
lineExplanations.48: Take the matching q position off the stack.
lineExplanations.49: If both positions are empty, they agree here.
lineExplanations.51: If exactly one is empty, the structures differ.
lineExplanations.52: Mark the trees as not the same.
lineExplanations.54: If both exist but their values differ…
lineExplanations.55: …mark the trees as not the same.
lineExplanations.63: Return whether the two trees are the same.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def isSameTree(self, p_values, q_values): the two trees as level-order arrays (null = missing node).
syntaxExplanations.6: pc = 1 starts p's child walker after the root.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(pn): loops over p's slots.
syntaxExplanations.8: p_values[i] is not None skips null slots.
syntaxExplanations.9: A dict literal with id, value, and None child links.
syntaxExplanations.10: pc < pn checks a slot is left for a left child.
syntaxExplanations.11: p_values[pc] is not None checks that slot is real.
syntaxExplanations.12: node["left"] = pc records the left child id.
syntaxExplanations.14: pc < pn checks a slot is left for a right child.
syntaxExplanations.15: p_values[pc] is not None checks that slot is real.
syntaxExplanations.16: node["right"] = pc records the right child id.
syntaxExplanations.19: p_by[i] = node stores it by id.
syntaxExplanations.23: qc = 1 starts q's child walker after the root.
syntaxExplanations.24: for i in range(qn): loops over q's slots.
syntaxExplanations.25: q_values[i] is not None skips null slots.
syntaxExplanations.26: A dict literal with id, value, and None child links.
syntaxExplanations.27: qc < qn checks a slot is left for a left child.
syntaxExplanations.28: q_values[qc] is not None checks that slot is real.
syntaxExplanations.29: node["left"] = qc records the left child id.
syntaxExplanations.31: qc < qn checks a slot is left for a right child.
syntaxExplanations.32: q_values[qc] is not None checks that slot is real.
syntaxExplanations.33: node["right"] = qc records the right child id.
syntaxExplanations.36: q_by[i] = node stores it by id.
syntaxExplanations.37: same = True is the running answer.
syntaxExplanations.38: done = False becomes True on the first difference.
syntaxExplanations.42: stack_p.append(0 if pn > 0 else None) seeds p's root.
syntaxExplanations.43: stack_q.append(0 if qn > 0 else None) seeds q's root.
syntaxExplanations.44: cur_p = None initializes the current p position.
syntaxExplanations.45: cur_q = None initializes the current q position.
syntaxExplanations.46: while len(stack_p) > 0 and not done: compare until done.
syntaxExplanations.47: cur_p = stack_p.pop() takes the next p position.
syntaxExplanations.48: cur_q = stack_q.pop() takes the matching q position.
syntaxExplanations.49: if cur_p is None and cur_q is None: both empty here.
syntaxExplanations.50: matched = matched + 1 counts the empty match.
syntaxExplanations.51: elif cur_p is None or cur_q is None: exactly one empty.
syntaxExplanations.57: else: both nodes exist and match (bare else — no step).
syntaxExplanations.58: matched = matched + 1 counts the matching pair.
syntaxExplanations.63: return same hands back the answer.
